_derivation.description: Real, server-computed HMAC-SHA256 fixture vectors proving byte-for-byte cross-language compatibility between Axiam\Sdk\Amqp\Hmac::verify (PHP) and crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs::sign_payload (Rust). Each 'valid' vector's hmac_signature was NOT hand-written -- it was emitted by the canonical Rust signer via a throwaway #[test] harness (emit_php_sdk_hmac_fixture, cargo test -p axiam-amqp --lib emit_php_sdk_hmac_fixture -- --nocapture) and the harness was reverted after use (never committed).
_derivation.reproduction_command: cd /home/user/axiam && (temporarily add an emit_php_sdk_hmac_fixture #[test] to crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs's tests module constructing the AuthzRequest/AuditEventMessage values below, print serde_json::to_vec(...) plus sign_payload(key, &bytes)) && cargo test -p axiam-amqp --lib emit_php_sdk_hmac_fixture -- --nocapture
_derivation.note: The field order in every 'message' object below is the Rust struct DECLARATION order (see AuthzRequest / AuditEventMessage in crates/axiam-amqp/src/messages.rs), NOT alphabetical order -- e.g. AuthzRequest emits tenant_id before subject_id, and action before resource_id. Hmac::verify's json_decode($body, true) + unset('hmac_signature') MUST preserve this exact insertion order (PHP associative arrays are ordered maps) -- ksort()/alphabetizing breaks every vector. 'authz_request_slash_nonascii_valid' is the Pitfall-1 regression case: its action field contains both a literal '/' and a non-ASCII character ('é'); it verifies true ONLY when the canonicalization json_encode() call passes JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES | JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE -- omitting either flag changes the canonical bytes and makes this specific vector (and only this one) go red, proving the regression is test-enforced.

vectors.name: authz_request_slash_nonascii_valid
vectors.note: Pitfall-1 regression: action contains a literal '/' and a non-ASCII 'é'. Verifies true only when Hmac::verify canonicalizes with JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES | JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE -- removing either flag must make this vector (and only this one) fail.

vectors.name: authz_request_tampered_action
vectors.note: Same signature as authz_request_valid but with action mutated from 'read' to 'write' after signing -- proves verification is non-vacuous.

vectors.name: audit_event_wrong_key
vectors.note: Same body/signature as audit_event_valid but verified against a different signing key (hex-decodes to 'wrong-key-for-test-only') -- proves the wrong key is rejected.

vectors.name: missing_hmac_signature
vectors.note: hmac_signature field entirely absent -- strict mode default (CONTRACT.md section 8.3) rejects, never silently accepts.

vectors.name: non_hex_signature
vectors.note: Non-hex signature value must be rejected without raising.

vectors.name: wrong_length_signature
vectors.note: Truncated (wrong-length) hex signature must be rejected without raising.

vectors.name: non_string_signature
vectors.note: Non-string hmac_signature (a JSON number) must be rejected without raising.
